title: NVR: A Good Jockey On A Strong Horse Riding Into A High Inflation Environment
text: In a high inflation environment, companies have to worry not only about higher costs. They also have to contend with the economic impact of the measures taken to control it. The homebuilding sector has the business economics to overcome the high inflation effects. But this requires you to view them through a cyclical lens. Think long term.

title: The Twilight Zone
text: Depending on your age, the bull market in stocks started in 1982, with stocks trading at six-times earnings and the U.S. Treasury Bonds paying 15% interest, or in 2009 at the bottom of the financial crisis. Either way, the combination of the Federal Reserve Board's liquidity and the popularity/stock market domination of technology stocks have entered us into what we will call “The Twilight Zone.

title: Home builder stocks shouldn't bounce much after earnings as rate outlook weighs, analyst says
text: J.P. Morgan analyst Michael Rehaut cut his price targets on a host of home builders' stocks, saying investors are likely to remain concerned about the sustainability of current demand given the continued rise in interest rates. The iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF ITB, +1.77% rose 2.0% in midday trading Wednesday, but has tumbled 29.4% since closing at a record $83.04 on Dec. 12, 2020.

title: Homebuilding Industry Driving Construction Spending: 4 Picks
text: Spending on residential construction projects is helping the construction sector, boding well for stocks like D.R. Horton (DHI), Lennar Corporation (LEN), Century Communities (CCS) and NVR, Inc (NVR).
